title: Zoro Refuses to Fall
description: On pages 12–15, zoro refuses to fall focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.
scene: On pages 12–15, zoro refuses to fall focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Hmm?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just as I thought... the wounds he received from Hawkeys still haven't...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: brother!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: This is my chance! THKOYHRI Punch!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hup,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Meet your Doom as you get crushed!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: RORONOA ZORO!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Aha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: nyu!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Aghhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Why don't you get crushed instead?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Damn it...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: of all the times, why now...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: his wounds are just too deep! they haven't healed over yet!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Obviously! It's a wound that would disable a man for at least half a year, if not kill him!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Was he enduring the pain all this time!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I knew something was up when he acted normal even after receiving those wounds!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: That moron!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I warned you before not to look somewhere else...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I'm a 40% level fishman-karate practitioner!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: What...? Did a human... just fly past us...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Brother Cook!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Brother Cook!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: They've been taken care of, along.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: What should we do with RORONOA zoro?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Just throw him into the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Couldn't even put up a fight... how boring.

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
